notes: timingOwnership is informational sequencing state, not a permanent lock policy.
sequence_agent must plan against the latest sequence revision.
sequenceSettings is required planner context because sequence-level settings such as supportsModelBlending materially affect rendering and may require explicit sequence.setSettings mutations.
displayElements provides the current explicit order state required for deterministic display-element reorder plans.
groupIds provides authoritative xLights group identity so group-first planning does not rely on name heuristics.
groupsById carries authoritative direct and flattened membership so nested-group planning can prefer the broadest valid aggregate target.
submodelsById carries authoritative parent linkage so parent/submodel overlap can collapse broad writes while preserving explicit refinement lines.

displayModelIndex is the compact project display/model index derived from the latest xLights layout refresh. It is the preferred structural source for target identity, fingerprints, custom model construction, node layout summaries, and enriched submodel metadata.

targetBehaviorEvidence carries compact project-local render/probe learning keyed by target or submodel fingerprint. It is advisory evidence for effect suitability, readability, and target/submodel behavior, not a replacement for current xLights readback.

Custom models and submodels use the same target framework as built-in models. The sequence agent should not infer user-specific prop meaning from target names or route custom models through a separate training bucket.
